input.topic: YellowKey Windows BitLocker bypass zero-day disclosure, May 2026

candidate_data.platform: Microsoft Windows 11 and Windows Server 2022/2025, per public researcher claims
candidate_data.exploit_status: Public proof-of-concept repository; physical access is required in the public reproduction path

input.notes: Manual Risky Bulletin intake from the May 13, 2026 bulletin. Draft conservatively from the Risky Bulletin, the public YellowKey repository, and independent reporting. Do not invent a CVE, Microsoft advisory, patch status, exploitation in the wild, remote attack path, or vendor confirmation. If Microsoft or NVD publishes a CVE before drafting, update the zero-day metadata from primary/vendor/government sources. Clearly distinguish the public physical-access reproduction path from any unproven remote or malware-assisted exploitation scenario.

preconditions: zero-day lane active count below 5 at discovery time

history.note: Manual Risky Bulletin link submission; pipeline-submit-link dry-run found no URL duplicates, but direct task JSON was used to avoid reusing branch-only task IDs from closed PR #699.
